description: Evaluate parallel float fetching optimization for trade-up scouting

evals.name: multi-input-trade-up-scout
evals.prompt: Scout trade-up opportunities under R20 and execute the top profitable one. Use budget of R20 and show unique outputs only. Pick the best opportunity and scout it. For multi-input trade-ups, the skill should spawn subagents in parallel to scout each input item simultaneously instead of sequentially.
evals.expected_output: Successfully scout a trade-up opportunity with multiple input items. The scouting should use parallel subagents (one per input item) to fetch float data concurrently. Report total items collected, pages accessed per item, and total time taken.

evals.name: single-vs-multi-trade-up
evals.prompt: Find and scout two trade-up opportunities: one Single-type (10x of same item) and one Multi-type (different items). For the Single type, pagination should run inline. For the Multi type, spawn subagents. Compare the scouting workflow used for each.
evals.expected_output: Single-type uses inline pagination loop. Multi-type spawns subagents (one per input group) that run in parallel. Report the different approaches clearly.

evals.name: parallel-subagent-coordination
evals.prompt: Scout a trade-up with 3+ input items under R30 budget. The skill should spawn one subagent per input item, all in parallel. Each subagent should handle pagination independently until goal or price ceiling. After all subagents complete, merge results and report total cost and items collected.
evals.expected_output: 3+ subagents spawn simultaneously and run in parallel. Each independently paginates its assigned item. After all complete, results merge cleanly with no duplicates or conflicts. Final report shows total items collected and unified cost breakdown.
